title: Calochlaena straminea
intro: Calochlaena straminea, also known as the stramineous fern, is a species of fern in the genus Calochlaena that is native to eastern Australia. The health benefits of Calochlaena straminea include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues and skin irritations. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Calochlaena straminea include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat a range of health issues, including coughs, colds, and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Calochlaena straminea is classified in the family Dryopteridaceae, which is a family of ferns. This family is part of the order Cyatheales, also known as the tree fern order. The order Cyatheales is further grouped into the class Polypodiopsida, which includes most modern ferns. The class Polypodiopsida is part of the division Pteridophyta, a division that includes all non-seed vascular plants. Calochlaena straminea is a member of the phylum Tracheophyta, which includes all vascular plants.
uses: Calochlaena straminea, also known as the Brisbane tree fern, has been traditionally used in Australian Aboriginal medicine for its various health benefits.
